h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: with the technician waiting that makes me want to

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I’m not sure what else I’m supposed to decide before it leaves.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: “worked” keeps me with the evidence on the table, while “meant” sends me guessing into the worker’s head.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with how to write the caption without inventing meaning.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: So test the verbs before print. “Meant” points inward, toward intention. “Worked” points outward, toward the public situation.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's concern about the slipperiness of 'job' by introducing a sharp grammatical/conceptual distinction between 'meant' and 'worked'.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Since there is no pivot learner turn, there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Since there is no pivot learner turn, there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: So test the verbs before print. “Meant” points inward, toward intention. “Worked” points outward, toward the public situation.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's concern about the slipperiness of 'job' by introducing a sharp grammatical/conceptual distinction between 'meant' and 'worked'.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: So test the verbs before print. “Meant” points inward, toward intention. “Worked” points outward, toward the public situation.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's concern about the slipperiness of 'job' by introducing a sharp grammatical/conceptual distinction between 'meant' and 'worked'.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to critique the shop's pricing mechanism as exploitation rather than rationing.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Put the facts in two columns.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's skepticism in L1 by shifting from conceptual lecturing to a structured analytical task.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Without a pivot learner turn, there is no opportunity for recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Without a pivot learner turn, there is no opportunity for recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Put the facts in two columns.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's skepticism in L1 by shifting from conceptual lecturing to a structured analytical task.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Put the facts in two columns.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor responds to the learner's skepticism in L1 by shifting from conceptual lecturing to a structured analytical task.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Fine, it is not a technicality; the drawing will not let me call it twice.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to simply compute the algebraic square of the new side length as prompted by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [draws one vertical split and one horizontal split through the larger outline.]
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts representation by drawing grid lines to help the learner visualize the 2x2 structure after the learner realizes their initial claim is wrong.
